title: Tender Chinese Pork Char-Siu Bao
description: Herbal, bouncy-qq, crowd-pleaser Chinese (Sichuan) dish featuring white bread mix, robata-grill, keto-friendly.
instructions: Cut the pork belly into 1-inch pieces and marinate for at least 30 minutes.
In a large bowl, combine the white bread mix, egg, sugar, light soy sauce, oyster sauce, and five-spice powder. Mix until just combined.
Add the marinated pork belly to the bread mix and mix until the meat is evenly coated.
Divide the dough into 8-10 equal pieces and shape each into a ball.
Roll out each ball into a thin circle, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place a tablespoon of the pork filling in the center of each circle and fold the dough over to form a half-moon shape.
Seal the edges by pressing with a fork and brush with a little water.
Grill the bao for 4-5 minutes on each side, or until golden brown.
Serve immediately with your favorite dipping sauce.
